text: Which Reactor type emits 0 or 1 item?
Which Reactor type emits 0 to N items?
What happens if no subscriber subscribes to a Flux?
options: It executes immediately and discards results

text: Which operator transforms each element of a Flux one-to-one?
What is the key difference between flatMap and concatMap?
options: flatMap preserves order; concatMap does not
concatMap preserves order; flatMap subscribes eagerly and may interleave
concatMap merges errors; flatMap does not

text: Which operator is used to share a single subscription among multiple subscribers?
What does Mono.defer() do compared to Mono.just()?
options: defer() caches the value; just() does not
defer() creates a new publisher for each subscriber; just() captures the value eagerly
defer() is only for error handling

text: Which Reactor operator switches to a fallback publisher on error?
What is backpressure in the context of Reactor?
options: A way to buffer all upstream items
A mechanism for a subscriber to signal how many items it can consume

text: What does the publishOn operator do?
options: Switches the Scheduler for upstream operators
Switches the Scheduler for downstream operators from that point
Runs the entire pipeline on a new thread
Same as subscribeOn but for Mono only

text: What does subscribeOn affect compared to publishOn?
options: subscribeOn affects operators after it; publishOn affects operators before it
subscribeOn sets the Scheduler for the source subscription and upstream; publishOn switches downstream from its position
They are interchangeable — both affect the full pipeline
subscribeOn only works with Mono; publishOn works with both

text:
What does this code print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .map(i -> i * 2)
  .filter(i -> i > 3)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Mono.just("hello")
  .map(String::toUpperCase)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What is the output of this code?
Flux.just("a", "b", "c")
  .take(2)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .flatMap(i -> Flux.just(i, i * 10))
  .subscribe(System.out::println);
options: Compilation error — flatMap expects a Mono

text:
What does this code print?
Mono.just(42)
  .flatMap(n -> Mono.empty())
  .defaultIfEmpty(-1)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .doOnNext(i -> System.out.print("seen:"))
  .map(i -> i + 10)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Mono.error(new RuntimeException("oops"))
  .onErrorReturn("fallback")
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .concatMap(i -> Flux.just(i, i * 10))
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Flux.range(1, 5)
  .filter(i -> i % 2 == 0)
  .reduce(0, Integer::sum)
  .subscribe(System.out::println);
In WebFlux, what does returning Mono<Void> from a @PostMapping method indicate?
options: The endpoint completes with no response body (e.g., 204 No Content)
The request is processed asynchronously with a callback

text: What is the role of WebFilter in Spring WebFlux?
options: It replaces @ExceptionHandler for reactive error handling
It intercepts and processes requests and responses in the reactive pipeline before reaching the handler
It is identical to a servlet Filter but runs on a thread pool
It only applies to WebSocket connections

text: How does Spring WebFlux handle a Flux<ServerSentEvent> returned from a controller?
options: It collects all events and sends them as a JSON array
It streams each event to the client as Server-Sent Events over a long-lived HTTP connection
It throws an error — Flux cannot be returned from controllers
It blocks until the Flux completes, then responds
Returns JSON array ["a","b","c"] when Flux completes
Blocks and returns only the first element
Compilation error — controllers must return ResponseEntity

text: What is the difference between RouterFunction and @RestController in WebFlux?
options: RouterFunction only works with functional endpoints; @RestController supports both styles
RouterFunction is the functional programming model using handler functions; @RestController is the annotation model — both are fully supported
RouterFunction is deprecated in favour of @RestController in WebFlux 3
RouterFunction requires blocking I/O; @RestController uses reactive I/O

text:
What does this code print?
Flux.just("x")
  .mergeWith(Flux.error(new RuntimeException("boom")))
  .onErrorReturn("err")
  .subscribe(System.out::println);

What does this code print?
Mono.just("start")
  .doOnSubscribe(s -> System.out.println("subscribed"))
  .map(v -> v + "!")
  .doOnNext(v -> System.out.println("next:" + v))
  .subscribe();

What does this code ultimately print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .switchMap(i -> Flux.just(i * 10))
  .subscribe(System.out::println);
Why is calling block() inside a Reactor pipeline dangerous?
options: block() is not allowed inside a Flux pipeline at compile time
It can cause a deadlock by blocking a non-blocking scheduler thread that is needed to complete the upstream
block() always returns null inside a pipeline
It converts the pipeline to synchronous mode permanently

text: What does Context.of() in Reactor provide that ThreadLocal cannot reliably provide?
options: Faster read access than ThreadLocal for high-throughput pipelines
A per-subscription immutable key-value store that propagates through the reactive chain without being tied to a specific thread
A way to share mutable state between concurrent subscribers
Automatic cleanup of resources when the pipeline completes

text:
What does this code print?
Flux.just(1, 2, 3)
  .map(i -> { if (i == 2) throw new RuntimeException(); return i; })
  .onErrorContinue((e, v) -> System.out.println("skip:" + v))
  .subscribe(System.out::println);
options: skip:2 then error terminates the flux

text: In Spring WebFlux, what happens if a @ExceptionHandler method returns Mono<ResponseEntity<?>>?
options: It causes an error — exception handlers must be synchronous
WebFlux subscribes to it and sends the response when the Mono completes, fully non-blocking
It is executed on a dedicated error-handling thread pool
The Mono is blocked immediately to extract the ResponseEntity
